updates.title: Complimentary domestic lounge access spend threshold updated
updates.summary: Effective 10 April 2026, AU Small Finance Bank has revised the complimentary domestic airport lounge access eligibility. Cardholders can now enjoy 2 complimentary domestic airport lounge visits per calendar quarter, provided they have spent a minimum of ₹50,000 in the previous calendar quarter.
updates.sourceLabel: AU Small Finance Bank official notice

tips.text: Link your AU ixigo RuPay variant with any UPI app to earn 1.25% rewards (5 reward points per Rs 200) on both online and offline UPI transactions.
The card features a standout 0% forex markup on international spends; however, note that international transactions do not earn reward points.

updates.title: BOBCARD Scapia Credit Card rewards and lounge access revised
updates.summary: Effective 27 February 2026, Scapia revised its reward eligibility and lounge threshold. Spends on insurance and utilities (including telecom) no longer earn Scapia Coins. Unlimited domestic lounge access and airport privileges now require a minimum spend of ₹20,000 in the preceding month (previously ₹10,000). International airport privileges now require booking an international flight of ₹50,000 or more via the Scapia app.

updates.title: Scapia Federal Credit Card rewards and lounge access revised
updates.summary: Effective 27 February 2026, Scapia revised its reward eligibility and lounge threshold. Spends on insurance and utilities (including telecom) no longer earn Scapia Coins. Unlimited domestic lounge access and airport privileges now require a minimum spend of ₹20,000 in the preceding month (previously ₹10,000). International airport privileges now require booking an international flight of ₹50,000 or more via the Scapia app.

updates.title: HDFC Infinia program retention criteria updated
updates.summary: Effective 1 April 2026, HDFC introduced new program retention criteria requiring cardholders to maintain a minimum of ₹18 Lakhs in card spends or ₹50 Lakhs in relationship value per financial year.

updates.title: HDFC Infinia fee exemptions clarified
updates.summary: HDFC officially clarified that Infinia cardholders are exempt from late payment charges, overlimit fees, cash advance fees, and reward/cashback redemption fees.

tips.text: Activate HDFC Global Value Program to get an additional 1% cashback on international spends.

Use the Finnair 1:1 Avios transfer workaround to transfer points to British Airways or Qatar Airways at a 1:1 ratio. Transfer to Finnair Plus (1:1) first, then link accounts to move those Avios 1:1 to British Airways or Qatar Airways, bypassing the direct 2:1 transfer ratio.

updates.title: HDFC revised Regalia Gold base rewards rate
updates.summary: Effective 15 May 2026, HDFC Bank has revised the base reward points earning rate to 5 reward points for every ₹200 spent on eligible transactions (previously 4 points per ₹150).

updates.title: HDFC revised domestic lounge access rules for Regalia Gold
updates.summary: Effective 1 July 2026, domestic lounge access is spend-gated. Cardholders must spend at least ₹60,000 in the preceding calendar quarter to unlock 3 complimentary visits for the next quarter.

updates.title: HDFC revised DCC markup fee
updates.summary: Effective 15 May 2026, HDFC Bank has revised its Dynamic Currency Conversion (DCC) markup fee to 1.75% on INR-denominated international transactions or transactions with merchants registered overseas.

updates.title: HDFC revised Regalia Gold rewards capping
updates.summary: Effective 1 July 2025, HDFC Bank revised the reward points capping on insurance spends to 2,000 reward points per calendar month. Grocery, utility, and telecom transactions remain capped at 2,000 reward points per calendar month.

tips.text: Activate HDFC Global Value Program to get an additional 1% cashback on international spends.
The best redemption option is transferring to Accor Live Limitless (ALL) at a 2:1 ratio. Since 1 Accor point is valued at Rs 2.2, this yields an effective value of Rs 1.1 per HDFC reward point.

updates.title: HDFC revised Diners Club Black rewards capping
updates.summary: Effective 1 July 2025, HDFC Bank revised the reward points capping on insurance spends to 5,000 reward points per calendar month. Grocery and utility transactions remain capped at 2,000 reward points per calendar month.

updates.title: HDFC revised DCC markup fee
updates.summary: Effective 15 May 2026, HDFC Bank has revised its Dynamic Currency Conversion (DCC) markup fee to 1.75% on INR-denominated international transactions or transactions with merchants registered overseas.

tips.text: Activate HDFC Global Value Program to get an additional 1% cashback on international spends.

Use the Finnair 1:1 Avios transfer workaround to transfer points to British Airways or Qatar Airways at a 1:1 ratio. Transfer to Finnair Plus (1:1) first, then link accounts to move those Avios 1:1 to British Airways or Qatar Airways, bypassing the direct 2:1 transfer ratio.

updates.title: American Express removed fuel rewards on Gold Card
updates.summary: Effective 12 June 2025, fuel payments no longer earn Membership Rewards points on the American Express Gold Card. Utility payments continue to earn 1 Membership Rewards point per Rs 50 spent, subject to the published monthly cap.

updates.title: HDFC revised Diners Privilege rewards, lounge access and DCC rules
updates.summary: Reward earnings moved to 4 reward points per Rs 200 on eligible categories from 15 May 2026. Lounge access becomes spend-gated from the quarter starting 1 July 2026, with 2 domestic and 1 international access per quarter available only after Rs 60,000 prior-quarter spend. The official change notice also revises INR DCC markup to 1.75% and the card reissuance fee to Rs 199.

tips.text: Activate HDFC Global Value Program to get an additional 1% cashback on international spends.

updates.title: IndusInd credit card charges revised
updates.summary: Effective 15 June 2026, IndusInd Bank has revised credit card charges. Late payment fees now range up to ₹1,300 depending on outstanding balance. Additionally, a 1% fee (plus GST) applies to Dynamic Currency Conversion (DCC) transactions, cumulative fuel transactions exceeding ₹30,000, and utility/transport payments exceeding ₹40,000 in a statement cycle.

updates.title: Complimentary airport lounge access discontinued
updates.summary: Effective 7 March 2025, IndusInd Bank discontinued all complimentary domestic and international airport lounge access benefits for the Legend credit card.

updates.title: Domestic Lounge access update
updates.summary: Effective 1st April, 2026 spends criteria tracking will be initiated to unlock complimentary domestic lounge access from July, 2026 quarter onwards.

updates.title: Golf benefit updates
updates.summary: Effective 10th March 2026, your golf benefit will now include 2 complimentary games and 2 complimentary lessons per month.

updates.title: IndusInd credit card charges revised
updates.summary: Effective 15 June 2026, IndusInd Bank has revised credit card charges. Late payment fees now range up to ₹1,300 depending on outstanding balance. Additionally, a 1% fee (plus GST) applies to Dynamic Currency Conversion (DCC) transactions, cumulative fuel transactions exceeding ₹50,000, and utility/transport payments exceeding ₹40,000 in a statement cycle.

updates.title: Domestic Lounge access update
updates.summary: Effective 1st April, 2026 spends criteria tracking of Rs 1.5 Lakh per quarter will be initiated to unlock complimentary domestic lounge access from July, 2026 quarter onwards.

updates.title: Golf and International Lounge updates
updates.summary: Effective 13th March 2025, your golf benefit includes 1 complimentary game and 1 complimentary lesson per month, and Priority Pass international lounge access is revised to 1 complimentary visit per quarter.

tips.text: The cash redemption value for Pinnacle is high at Rs 0.75 per point, but cash redemption is capped at 7,500 points per calendar month, and you can only redeem up to 50% of your total points balance at once.

updates.title: IndusInd credit card charges revised
updates.summary: Effective 15 June 2026, IndusInd Bank has revised credit card charges. Late payment fees now range up to ₹1,300 depending on outstanding balance. Additionally, a 1% fee (plus GST) applies to Dynamic Currency Conversion (DCC) transactions, cumulative fuel transactions exceeding ₹50,000, and utility/transport payments exceeding ₹40,000 in a statement cycle.

updates.title: Axis revised Airtel Axis Bank Credit Card features and terms
updates.summary: Effective 12 April 2026, Axis ties accelerated cashback more tightly to regular spending: the monthly cap on 25% and 10% cashback is limited to twice the base cashback earned in the same statement month.

updates.title: Axis revised Travel EDGE transfer program for Atlas
updates.summary: Effective 2 April 2026, Axis Bank revised its Travel EDGE transfer program: Accor Live Limitless, Marriott Bonvoy, and Qatar Airways were removed as transfer partners. Three new airline partners (British Airways, Finnair, Vietnam Airlines) were added at a 2:1 transfer ratio, while legacy partners continue to transfer at 1:2 ratio. Annual caps of 30,000 EDGE Miles for Group A and 120,000 EDGE Miles for Group B apply.

tips.text: Air India has recently revalued its redemptions and is classified under Group B (subject to the 120,000 annual EDGE Mile transfer cap).
For Marriott Bonvoy transfers, you can indirectly transfer by converting EDGE Miles to Club ITC at a 1:2 ratio, and then Club ITC to Marriott at a 2:3 ratio (capped at 10,000 ITC points per quarter).
Air India has recently revalued its redemptions and is classified under Group B (subject to the 120,000 annual EDGE Mile transfer cap).
For Marriott Bonvoy transfers, you can indirectly transfer by converting EDGE Miles to Club ITC at a 1:2 ratio, and then Club ITC to Marriott at a 2:3 ratio (capped at 10,000 ITC points per quarter).
Air India is classified under Group B (subject to the 400,000 annual EDGE Reward Points transfer cap at a 5:2 ratio) and has recently revalued its redemptions.

For Marriott Bonvoy transfers, you can indirectly transfer by converting EDGE Reward Points to Club ITC at a 5:2 ratio, and then Club ITC to Marriott at a 2:3 ratio (capped at 10,000 ITC points per quarter).

updates.title: Axis revised Magnus for Burgundy transfer partners
updates.summary: Effective 2 April 2026, Axis Bank revised its transfer partner program for Magnus for Burgundy: Accor Live Limitless, Marriott Bonvoy, and Qatar Airways were removed as transfer partners. Three new airline partners (British Airways, Finnair, Vietnam Airlines) were added at a 5:2 transfer ratio, while legacy partners continue to transfer at a 5:4 ratio. Annual caps of 2,00,000 EDGE REWARD Points for Group A and 8,00,000 EDGE REWARD Points for Group B apply.

updates.title: Axis revised Magnus for Burgundy rewards and spend exclusions
updates.summary: Effective 20 June 2025, Magnus for Burgundy continues to earn 12 EDGE REWARD Points per Rs 200 up to Rs 1.5 lakh of monthly eligible spends and 35 EDGE REWARD Points per Rs 200 above that threshold. Axis also simplified exclusions by spend category and states that utility and telecom, wallet, government, insurance, gold and jewellery, fuel, cash advances, and repayments are excluded from reward earn and spend-based fee waiver calculation, while rent remains capped at Rs 50,000 per month.

updates.title: Axis added EDGE points forfeiture rule for closed or overdue cards
updates.summary: Effective 1 October 2025, Axis may forfeit unredeemed EDGE REWARD Points or EDGE Miles 30 days after card closure, or when the minimum amount due remains outstanding for more than 90 days.

tips.text: Air India is classified under Group B (subject to the 800,000 annual EDGE Reward Points transfer cap at a 5:4 Burgundy ratio) and has recently revalued its redemptions.

For Marriott Bonvoy transfers, you can indirectly transfer by converting EDGE Reward Points to Club ITC at a 5:4 ratio, and then Club ITC to Marriott at a 2:3 ratio (capped at 10,000 ITC points per quarter).
Air India is classified under Group B (subject to the 400,000 annual EDGE Reward Points transfer cap at a 5:2 ratio) and has recently revalued its redemptions.

For Marriott Bonvoy transfers, you can indirectly transfer by converting EDGE Reward Points to Club ITC at a 5:2 ratio, and then Club ITC to Marriott at a 2:3 ratio (capped at 10,000 ITC points per quarter).

updates.title: Promotional 0% forex markup offer active
updates.summary: HSBC RuPay Cashback Credit Card currently offers a promotional 0% forex markup on international transactions valid until 30 June 2026. Outside this promotional window, the standard 3.5% markup applies.
